extDesc.message: Reclaim your own personal data from services that lock it behind non-automatable walls — within your own session.

about_body.message: Habeas extracts your own data — receipts, invoices, transactions — from services with no API and no email export, entirely within your own logged-in session. It never stores your credentials, and nothing is sent anywhere you don't choose.

manage_accounts_hint.message: Choose which of this source's accounts to import. Only the selected accounts are offered for listing, auto and sync. Saved on the source.

accounts_pick_sub.message: Only the checked accounts are offered for download (listing, auto and sync).

accounts_failed.message: Could not load accounts (log in to the site first?).

full_history_hint.message: Re-list everything fresh from the site so the selected documents can be re-downloaded (rows loaded from the local archive are record-only and are NOT re-fetched). Requires being logged in to the site.

force_redownload_hint.message: Re-fetch the selected documents' files + details from the site (even rows loaded from the local archive), refreshing the real date/amount in the archive. Requires being logged in.

pill_new_hint.message: Not yet delivered to the selected destination — it will be included in the next send.

pill_sent_hint.message: Already delivered to the selected destination.

no_datasources.message: Activate a data source in Settings.

capture_hint.message: Open the site and your purchases page to capture your session, then retry.

login_in_tab.message: Log in to the site in the tab we just opened, then try again.

login_wait.message: Log in to the site in the tab we opened — listing resumes automatically once your session is detected.

challenge_solve.message: The site showed an anti-bot check — solve it in the tab we opened, then List again.

cookies_cleared.message: Cleared $N$ site cookies for a clean login.

cookies_cleared_login.message: Cleared $N$ site cookies — log in in the new tab, then test again.

listing_year_page.message: Listing documents from $Y$, page $P$ ($N$ so far)

retrieve_failed.message: Could not retrieve the file from this destination.

delivered_nofile.message: Delivered here (record only — no individual file)

pick_sink.message: Set up a destination in Settings.

configure_folder.message: Set the destination folder in Settings.

fs_unsupported.message: This browser can’t pick a folder (no File System Access). Use Downloads or Drive.

auto_help.message: Runs when YOU log in to the site and sends only what's new. Drive/HTTP destinations only (local ones need the page open).

device_enter_code.message: On the opened page, enter code: $CODE$

dest_is_archive_hint.message: This is the same connection your archive is kept on (chosen under “Where they’re kept”). It’s one connection playing two roles — not a duplicate.

set_default_hint.message: Pre-select this destination for sources without their own, and use it in Sync all.

sink_name_hint.message: A friendly, visible name for this destination (falls back to the id)

sink_rename_hint.message: Set a friendly, visible name for this destination

sink_rename_prompt.message: Visible name for this destination (blank to clear):

enable_ds_first.message: Enable a data source to configure auto.

add_sink_first.message: Add a Drive or HTTP destination first.

redirect_hint.message: Empty = use Habeas's client. Redirect URI to register in Google Console (with trailing slash):

email_hint.message: Sends one email per batch with the documents attached, using YOUR provider account — the key is stored encrypted and data goes straight to the provider. The sender must be verified with that provider. API key: Resend/Postmark/Brevo/SMTP2GO/Mailgun. Domain: Mailgun only. Access key + secret + region: Amazon SES only.

dbx_hint.message: Add the sink, then click “Connect Dropbox” to authorize (one click). Advanced/Firefox fallback: paste your own app key + a refresh token.

none_compatible.message: No compatible documents for this destination.

st_challenged.message: anti-bot check — open the site and solve it, then retry

log_err_auth.message: $SOURCE$ needs you signed in — open it so Habeas can use the live session.

log_err_token_expired.message: Habeas’s saved access to $SOURCE$ had expired (banks issue very short-lived tokens, even while your login stays active). Open $SOURCE$ so a fresh one is captured.

log_err_auth_valid.message: $SOURCE$ rejected the request even though the access token was still valid — likely a temporary block or a missing session detail, not an expired login. See the technical details.

log_err_token_missing.message: No valid $SOURCE$ access token went out with this request — Habeas may not have a current one captured. Open $SOURCE$ (signed in) so a fresh token is captured, then try again.

log_err_gone.message: The service refused the request (code $N$) — the document may no longer be available.

log_err_server.message: The service had a problem (code $N$). It’s usually temporary — try again later.

status_challenged.message: $NAME$: anti-bot check — open the site and solve it

group_pick_sub.message: This source has several accounts. Pick one, or all.

notify_challenge.message: $NAME$: solve the anti-bot check we just opened, then it will sync.

consent_reads.message: Reads your data in your own logged-in session on: $HOSTS$

consent_replays.message: Replays that session to the service API: $HOST$

consent_offsite_warn.message: ⚠ This source also sends your session to a DIFFERENT domain: $HOSTS$. Only enable it if you trust it.

needs_consent.message: Enable this source in Settings first (it needs your consent).

authz_intro.message: A website is asking Habeas to set up a data flow. Nothing runs until you allow it.

authz_note.message: The destination is on the requesting site's own domain — your data can only go back to it. You can revoke this anytime in Settings.

authz_ls_intro.message: A website wants to see which data sources you currently have enabled in Habeas. It will receive only the source names and categories, never your accounts, documents, or data.

authz_ls_note.message: This reveals the list of enabled sources to the requesting site. You can revoke it anytime in Settings, Site integrations.

authz_granted.message: Allowed — you can close this window.

authz_expired.message: This request has expired. Ask the site to try again.

grants_help.message: Sites you've allowed to trigger collection to their own domain. Revoke any at any time.

author_intro.message: Record a new source from a site you use: Habeas watches your own session as you browse your data, then drafts a source you can test, save, and share. Nothing leaves your browser.

author_tips_title.message: For a complete capture, in this order:

author_tip_pages.message: If your list spans several pages, browse a few of them (page 2, 3…) so Habeas sees the pagination.

author_tip_detail.message: Then open the detail of one item — that captures the per-item data endpoint.

author_tip_pdf.message: If you want the PDFs, download one invoice/receipt from its detail page during capture.

author_tip_wait.message: Give each page a second to load its data before moving on, then come back and Analyze.

author_note_advanced.message: Heads-up: capture doesn't work on every site. Some — heavily protected SPAs or advanced anti-bot walls (seen on certain retailers) — don't expose their data to record mode, and nothing gets captured. If that happens you can still build the source by hand: open the site's DevTools → Network, find the request that returns your data, and use "Write/paste JSON" (in Settings) to define it — like the shipped sources were made.

author_denied.message: Access to that site was denied.

author_learning.message: Learning from $DOMAIN$ — browse your data, then Analyze.

author_start_first.message: Start learning from a site first.

author_no_samples.message: No responses captured yet — browse your data on the site, then retry.

author_no_requests.message: The recorder didn't see any requests. Reload the extension, re-open the site, accept the access prompt, then browse your orders and retry.

author_ws_captured.message: Captured $N$ realtime frames from $S$ WebSocket/SSE connection(s). This site streams its data (not plain HTTP) — auto-drafting isn't available yet, but the recording holds what a maintainer needs to build the source. Share it.

author_live_recording.message: Recording — just browse your orders / invoices as usual

author_guide_title.message: The team asked you to record this

author_guide_done.message: Got what the team needs — you can send now

author_live_hint_empty.message: No data yet. Open your orders / invoices page so the site loads it — and scroll down to load them all.

author_live_hint_draft.message: ✅ Found your data ($D$ documents). Press “Analyze” to build the draft — or just open one document so we learn how to download it.

author_live_hint_maintainer.message: ✅ Captured your data ($D$ documents) over a special transport ($T$). This can't be auto-built here, but the recording is complete — share it and a maintainer will turn it into a source.

author_share_note.message: Personal values (names, addresses, amounts, account/card numbers, tokens) are replaced with placeholders like [text] / [id]. No login tokens, cookies, or page text are included. Sending goes to the Habeas team, who may reply with questions in Settings → My contributions.

author_share_done.message: Downloaded an anonymized copy ($S$ requests, $W$ realtime frames) — it contains no personal data or credentials.

author_sent.message: Sent — thank you! Ref $ID$. Follow it in Settings → My contributions; the team may reply with questions.

author_sent_recording.message: Sent to the team — thank you! It's added to your existing conversation; you can close this tab.

author_recording_note.message: 📎 Recording sent to the team — for the requested capture.

author_send_fail.message: Couldn't send ($E$). You can use “Download a copy” and send it another way.

author_orphan_intro.message: A few ids couldn't be traced to anything else in your session. They're likely non-personal system/entity ids the maintainer needs to build the source — but they could be personal. Tick only the ones you're OK sharing the real value of; the rest stay hidden.

contrib_help.message: Recordings you sent to the Habeas team. Follow their status and answer any questions so your source can be built — you're credited when it ships.

contrib_empty.message: You haven't sent any recordings yet. Record a site (Author a source) and press “Send to the Habeas team”.

contrib_install_hint.message: the team built this source from your recording — install it to test

contrib_installed.message: ✓ Installed — open the Habeas popup and press List

contrib_capreq_title.message: The team needs one more recording

contrib_report_prefix.message: ⚠️ It didn't work when I tried the source.

contrib_report_none.message: no technical details right now — the team will just know it didn't work

contrib_report_fail.message: couldn't send — check your connection and try again

contrib_notify.message: The Habeas team replied to one of your contributions — see Settings → My contributions.

contrib_popup_notice.message: 💬 The Habeas team replied to your contribution(s) — open Settings → My contributions.

author_no_list_seen.message: Saw $N$ requests (from: $HOSTS$) but none returned a document list. Open your orders/invoices page so it loads the data, then retry — or tell us these hosts.

author_no_list.message: Couldn't find a document list in the captured responses.

author_detected.message: Detected list “$ITEMS$” ($COUNT$ items). Review the mapping below.

author_crossdomain.message: Off-site data host: $HOSTS$ — will ask for consent.

author_no_auth.message: No captured session for this API yet — browse your data on the site first.

via_table.message: an HTML table on the page

author_completing_sub.message: Record the product this source is missing (e.g. a card). Drafting then ADDS it as a new stream — you contribute the declarative shape, never your data.

author_augmented.message: Added stream “$SID$” to $ID$ — review and share it as a PR.

je_no_tab.message: Tip: open the site in a tab and log in, so the test runs in your session.

search_sources.message: Search sources by name, service, category…

store_help.message: Where your extracted data is kept. Sinks and consumers are served from it — so a second sink, device or record-only consumer reuses it instead of re-extracting. Choose local, a synced folder, or your own HTTP endpoint, and move it freely.

dest_multidevice_hint.message: Syncing across devices? Use a native cloud backend the extension writes directly — Google Drive, Dropbox, WebDAV or S3 — as both archive and sink. Don't point the local-folder option at a folder synced by an external tool (Google Drive Desktop, grive2, rclone…): the extension can't dedup against files another app created (and with Drive's drive.file scope it can't even see them), which duplicates folders. A native backend is the same app on every device, so it dedups across them.

store_no_sink.message: — add a sink of this type first —

store_need_sink.message: Pick a configured sink for the archive.

market_intro.message: Browse sources shared by the community. Each one is reviewed JSON — data, not code — and is confined to its own site's domain. Sources marked with a warning also send your session off-site; enable those only if you trust them.

market_unavailable.message: Registry unavailable (offline or not published yet).

market_beta_hint.message: Experimental — drafted but not yet verified against a real account. It may not work; installing helps test it.

market_beta_confirm.message: “$SOURCE$” is experimental and hasn't been verified against a real account yet. It may not work. Install it and help test it?

market_complete_hint.message: Record a product this source is missing (a card, an investment) and add it as a new stream.

market_gaps_hint.message: Products this source does not cover yet — install it and use “Complete” to add one you have.

market_updates_available.message: $N$ update(s) available for your installed sources.

author_map_intro.message: Habeas detected your data. Check each field below shows the right example, then Test and Save. You rarely need “Advanced”.

author_find_none.message: Not found in the captured data — try another value, or open the exact page/order first.

author_find_ok.message: Found in a list of $N$ items — mapping below.

author_find_multi.message: Found in $N$ lists — pick the right one below.

author_multistream.message: This site has several data sets — capture them all together

author_list_hint.message: Totals across the pages you browsed (items · pages captured). When you use the source it paginates to fetch them all — you pick the right list here, not pages.

planner_help.message: Schedule periodic downloads from a source to a destination. They run in your live browser session — if you're not signed in when a schedule fires, Habeas opens the site so you can sign in, then retries.

sched_desc_monthlybusiness.message: The $p1$ business day of each month $p2$

notify_sched_login.message: $p1$: a scheduled download needs you to sign in — we opened the site.

notify_sched_gaveup.message: $p1$: a scheduled download couldn't complete after retries; it will try again at the next scheduled time.

archive_index_sub.message: Every document you have recovered, organised by source.

archive_empty_sub.message: Recover documents from a source in the popup and they will appear here.

archive_no_dest.message: This document is in your archive but not yet copied to a destination. Use “Save to” above to copy this source’s pending documents to Drive, a folder, or an HTTP consumer.

archive_delivered_data.message: This is a movement — its data was delivered inside the manifest, so there is no separate file to open.

archive_file_missing.message: This document has a file (e.g. a PDF), but it isn’t available here yet — it wasn’t fetched at send time (for example the source’s session failed). Re-sync the source to download it.

archive_file_notyet.message: This document has a file (e.g. a PDF) that hasn’t been fetched yet. Send it to a destination to download and keep the file.

archive_save_hint.message: Save this source’s pending documents to $S$

archive_save_nosession.message: Sign in to $S$ (open it) and try again

quick_sub.message: $N$ sources in your archive · sync to pull whats new

archive_pick_account_sub.message: This source has $N$ accounts — choose one in the left panel to see its documents.

archive_refresh_nosession.message: Sign in to $NAME$ in the tab that opened, then refresh again

archive_refresh_challenge.message: $NAME$ needs a quick verification — complete it in the open tab, then retry

archive_accounts_hint.message: Choose which accounts this source tracks

archive_country_hint.message: Manage this source's country instances — each keeps its own archive and schedule

archive_country_sub.message: Pick the countries you use. Each becomes its own instance with a separate archive and schedule, so their documents never mix. One country = a single source.

popup_advanced_note.message: Everything here now lives in the Archive. These controls stay for edge cases and record mode.

quick_sub_empty.message: Your recovered documents live here. Open the Archive to add a source and pull your data.

archive_refresh_full_hint.message: Re-scan the entire history, not just new documents

archive_load_store_hint.message: Reload from the local archive — no network request

archive_reconcile_hint.message: Read the real data (dates, amounts, details) from what was already delivered (no re-download) and save it to the archive

fmt_unconfirmed.message: Not confirmed yet — this document may not have this file. Scan file formats to check.

archive_scan_hint.message: Check the destination for which file formats each document actually has, so a format a document lacks (e.g. no invoice PDF) stops offering its button

archive_scan_none.message: Nothing to scan (formats already known, or no delivered documents)

archive_scan_pending.message: $1 delivered document(s) haven't had their file formats checked yet.

archive_reconcile_running.message: Recovering real data from the destination…

archive_reconcile_none.message: No richer data found in the destination

archive_reconcile_nods.message: This source isn't configured as a datasource

archive_status_local_hint.message: The data is in your archive; the file itself is still pending — not yet saved to your destination.

archive_legend.message: “Pending” = we have the data in your archive, but the file itself isn't saved yet. “Saved” = the file is saved to your destination.

onboard_body.message: Habeas recovers your documents into your archive — where everything it collects is kept. Right now that archive lives only in this browser.

onboard_cloud.message: To reach your archive from more than one device or browser, keep it in the cloud. We recommend Dropbox — Google Drive’s app-scoped access makes it awkward for this.

onboard_add_sub.message: Set up a destination. If it can host your archive, your archive will be moved there.

onboard_sink_added.message: Destination added. (It stores delivered documents; it can’t host the archive itself.)

opt_home_intro.message: Set up where Habeas gets your documents, where they’re kept, and when they refresh.

opt_step_services_sub.message: Choose where to recover documents from (your supermarket, your bank…).

opt_step_storage.message: Choose where your documents are kept

opt_step_storage_sub.message: In this browser, or in the cloud (to have them on several devices).

opt_step_auto_sub.message: Let Habeas pull what’s new for you.

opt_ov_store_vs_p.message: Your single catalog of everything Habeas extracts — the data of each document (date, amount, merchant, category…), kept in one place you choose (this browser, a folder, or the cloud). It fills automatically as you list a source. It holds the records and tracks where each file went — not the original PDF/Excel files themselves.

opt_ov_dests_vs_p.message: Where the actual document files are sent when you send them. “Download” saves them to your computer; a folder or the cloud keeps them there (and Habeas can show them back to you from it). You can have several — the archive above just remembers what was delivered where.

opt_services_intro.message: Connect the services you want to recover documents from. You sign in yourself; Habeas never stores your password.

opt_services_adv_help.message: Create your own source, or import one shared with you.

opt_storage_intro.message: Your documents live in your archive. Keep it in the cloud to have them on all your devices, and safe.

store_import_help.message: If you already have documents saved to a destination, bring them into your archive without re-downloading.

opt_auto_intro.message: Let Habeas pull new documents for you, without opening it every time.

opt_privacy_intro.message: How Habeas protects your data, and what you’ve allowed.

opt_privacy_p1.message: Habeas never stores your passwords — you sign in, in your own session.

opt_privacy_p2.message: Nothing leaves your browser until you choose a destination.

opt_privacy_p3.message: It’s open source (AGPL-3.0): anyone can audit what it does.

opt_advanced_intro.message: Create your own sources and manage your contributions.

opt_dests_help.message: Beyond your archive, you can send copies of your documents to a folder, the cloud, or an app.

opt_dests_subtitle.message: Places Habeas can send copies of your documents — a folder, the cloud, or a connected app. Rename them, or mark one as the default. Your archive is always kept separately.

opt_dests_add_help.message: Add somewhere new to send copies to. You can give it a friendly name now, or rename it later.

archive_preview_fail.message: Couldn’t load this file from its destination. If it was delivered by an old build, try Re-download from site.

archive_send_notfound.message: Those documents weren’t found in your archive.

archive_send_incompat.message: $NAME$ doesn’t accept those documents (its category filter).
